title: Transactions, Isolation, Concurrency, and Recovery · Integrity Triage
summary: Two operators reassign and resolve the same ticket while a failure occurs between related writes. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a concurrency-safe ticket transition protocol and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain atomicity, consistency, isolation, and durability using a multi-statement business invariant.
Bound a unit of work with BEGIN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, and savepoints and verify failure behavior.
Recognize dirty read, nonrepeatable read, phantom, lost update, and write-skew schedules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Recovery Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
Order the Recovery Backup Restore evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Recovery Backup Restore.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Recovery Backup Restore assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Recovery Backup Restore, keep Recovery Backup Restore connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: Operators A and B read ticket 103 as open; A assigns it while B resolves it and a failure occurs between event insert and status update. Identify the anomaly, transaction boundary, retry precondition, and restore evidence.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Recovery Backup Restore has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Order the regression proof · Debug Recovery Backup Restore, name the row grain, Recovery Backup Restore precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Order the regression proof · Debug Recovery Backup Restore, trace this concrete input and predict rows or changes before running it: Operators A and B read ticket 103 as open; A assigns it while B resolves it and a failure occurs between event insert and status update. Identify the anomaly, transaction boundary, retry precondition, and restore evidence.
For Order the regression proof · Debug Recovery Backup Restore, challenge the documented misconception with result, constraint, or plan evidence: A replica is automatically a complete backup.

options.text: Declare stakeholder question, input relations, row grain, keys, NULL meaning, and expected cardinality for the debug Recovery Backup Restore case
Predict relational transformations, constraint or transaction effects, and dialect boundary before execution for the debug Recovery Backup Restore case
Run the smallest browser SQL statement and retain result rows, changed count, error, or query plan for the debug Recovery Backup Restore case
Reconcile totals and invariants, test changed data, and document security, recovery, and portability limits for the debug Recovery Backup Restore case

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Distinguish rollback, crash recovery, backup, replication, and tested point-in-time restoration.
